news.summary: On June 6, 2025, Wayne-based Mr. Splash Car Wash revealed a significant collaboration with NBA stars Giannis Antetokounmpo, Thanasis Antetokounmpo, and Michael Carter-Williams. This partnership involves a strategic alliance between Mr. Splash, a company under Argosy Private Equity, and the three basketball players. The details of this collaboration, including its scope and specific initiatives, were highlighted in the announcement.
news.headline: Mr. Splash Car Wash forms partnership with NBA stars Giannis & Thanasis Antetokounmpo, and Michael Carter-Williams.

news.summary: Soligenix, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company listed as Nasdaq: SNGX, based in Princeton, New Jersey, recently emphasized the contributions of Ellen Kim, M.D., who serves as the Lead Principal Investigator for their Phase 3 FLASH (1 and 2) studies. These studies focus on early-stage cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL), particularly mycosis fungoides (MF), which is the predominant type of CTCL. The company is dedicated to developing and launching products aimed at addressing rare diseases with unmet medical needs. This specific endeavor highlights the advancement of HyBryte™, a synthetic hypericin, as a potential novel therapy for individuals affected by MF.
news.headline: Soligenix highlights Dr. Ellen Kim's work on Phase 3 FLASH studies for HyBryte in CTCL patients - potential new therapy.

news.summary: Today, Bango released its financial results for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024, indicating a comprehensive overview of its performance during this period. Additionally, the company has shared insights on its current trading status and has outlined its outlook for the year 2025. This update includes crucial figures and data showcasing Bango's progress and future trajectory in the market.
news.headline: Bango reports 2024 full year results & provides outlook for 2025, ended on 31 Dec. Keep followers updated on trading.

news.summary: 📊 The Monetary Authority of Singapore has implemented a ruling that restricts the majority of cryptocurrency companies from exclusively catering to overseas customers without a license. The authority stated that such licenses will be rarely issued.
news.headline: Singapore imposes strict regulations on foreign-only digital token services, requiring a license for operation, with few being issued.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the number of new COVID-19 cases has decreased by approximately 30% in the past week. The total number of active cases currently stands at around 50,000, with 2,000 patients in critical condition. At the same time, the number of recoveries has reached 100,000. The government has announced that they will be increasing testing capacity to 100,000 tests per day in an effort to combat the spread of the virus. Additionally, vaccination efforts continue with over 1 million doses administered so far. The public is urged to remain vigilant and adhere to safety guidelines to prevent further outbreaks.
news.headline: US employment increases by 139,000 in May, showing a slowing trend.

news.summary: Deerfield, Illinois - On June 6, 2025, BAB, Inc. disclosed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly distribution of $0.01 per share for shareholders. The payment will be made on July 14, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of June 26, 2025. BAB, Inc. is listed on OTCQB under the ticker symbol BABB.
news.headline: BAB, Inc. (OTCQB: BABB) declares $0.01 quarterly cash distribution per share, payable on July 14, 2025 to shareholders of record as of June 26, 2025.

news.summary: A recent study revealed that 90% of taxi drivers identify as football enthusiasts, but a staggering 91% opt to work instead of watching important games live. This decision costs them over £200 in potential earnings each time they prioritize their passion for football over work. In light of this, Carlsberg is launching a campaign to show appreciation for taxi drivers by providing support or incentives.
news.headline: Carlsberg surprises taxi drivers during UEFA Nations League Finals with loss of £200 earnings per game.

news.summary: The global rapid drug screen market is estimated to be valued at USD 2.4 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 8.1%, reaching USD 5.1 billion by 2034. The market growth is driven by increasing demand for efficient drug testing methods in various sectors. Workplace drug testing is a key driver, with corporations prioritizing drug-free policies. Technological advancements, including AI integration, have enhanced testing speed and accuracy. Regulatory support and public health awareness are also boosting market demand. However, challenges include regional regulatory variations and product accuracy concerns. The market is segmented by product type, sample type, end-user, application, and technology. Immunoassay-based tests dominate the market, accounting for 60% of revenue. Urine tests hold a 70% market share by sample type, while hospitals are the largest end-user segment, representing 50% of the market. North America leads geographically, contributing 48% of the global market share.
news.headline: Global rapid drug screen market to reach USD 5.1 billion by 2034, growing at 8.1% CAGR from 2024 value of USD 2.4 billion. Major growth drivers: workplace drug testing demand, AI integration, regulatory support. Challenges include regulatory variability, accuracy of kits, and supply chain vulnerabilities. Market segmented by product type, sample type, end-user, application, and technology. North America dominates with 48% market share.

news.summary: The global medical nano coatings market was valued at $1.8 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow to $4.5 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 9.4%. This growth is driven by advancements in nanotechnology, increasing demand for infection-resistant surfaces, and rising healthcare expenditures. Nano coatings are applied in medical devices, implants, and diagnostic equipment. Key drivers include the prevalence of healthcare-associated infections, technological innovation, and increasing investments in healthcare infrastructure. Silver-based coatings are prominent for their antimicrobial properties, while Titanium dioxide coatings are gaining traction for implants. Polymer-based coatings offer customizability and suitability for various medical devices. The market is segmented by materials (silver, titanium dioxide, polymers), applications (medical devices, surgical instruments, implants), technologies (PVD, CVD, ALD, Sol-Gel), end-users (hospitals, ASCs, research labs), and regions (North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, Middle East & Africa). North America leads the market with a 45% share, followed by Europe with 30%, with countries like the US, Germany, the UK, and France leading in innovation and adoption.
news.headline: Global medical nano coatings market expected to reach $4.5 billion by 2034, growing at a 9.4% CAGR. Silver-based coatings dominate with antimicrobial efficacy. North America leads with 45% market share followed by Europe at 30%.
STOCKS MOSTLY POSITIVE ON WEEK

news.summary: The global mobile G-arm market was valued at around USD 1.5 billion in 2024 and is forecasted to reach close to USD 2.8 billion by 2034, with a projected compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.7% from 2025 to 2034. The increased utilization of advanced imaging technologies in surgical and diagnostic procedures is fueling this growth, as healthcare providers integrate mobile G-arm systems for enhanced precision and improved patient outcomes. Mobile G-arm systems account for 60% of the market, followed by imaging equipment at 25% and accessories at 15%. Orthopedic surgery is the largest application segment at 40%, followed by trauma surgery at 25%, neurological surgery at 15%, and interventional radiology at 20%. Hospitals are the primary end-users, representing over 70% of the demand, while outpatient surgical centers and diagnostic imaging centers make up smaller portions of the market. The Asia-Pacific region leads the market with a 45% revenue share in 2024, driven by technological advancements, growing elderly populations, and increased investments in healthcare infrastructure in countries like China and India. North America is the second-largest market, with a 30% share in 2024 and a projected CAGR of 6% through 2034.
news.headline: Global mobile G-arm market valued at USD 1.5B in 2024, projected to reach USD 2.8B by 2034, growing at a 6.7% CAGR due to increased demand in surgical and diagnostic applications. Dominated by mobile G-arm systems, it includes imaging equipment and accessories with North America and Asia-Pacific leading the market. Orthopedic surgery holds the largest market share of 40%, with hospitals as primary end-users. Digital mobile G-arm technology dominates with an 85% share. Asia-Pacific region commands a 45% revenue share in 2024.

news.summary: The Administrative Regional Court of Latvia issued a ruling on June 5, 2025, concluding a decade-long case regarding the legality of the Latvian Competition Council's decision on August 7, 2014, involving TKM Grupp AS and subsidiary KIA Auto AS. Despite presenting extensive explanations and evidence, the court upheld the Competition Authority's decision, prompting KIA Auto AS and TKM Grupp AS to express disappointment. The companies plan to challenge the judgment by filing a cassation claim with the Supreme Court of Latvia. Initial announcement of the case was made on August 21, 2014.
news.headline: Latvian court upholds decision against KIA Auto AS & TKM Grupp AS after 10-year case. Companies to appeal.

news.summary: Children's Place, Inc. (NASDAQ: PLCE) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on June 6, with analysts forecasting a loss of 51 cents per share. The company, based in Secaucus, New Jersey, is anticipated to report quarterly revenue of $260.62 million, a decrease from $267.88 million compared to the previous year. In the fourth quarter, the company experienced a 10.2% year-over-year decline in net sales, dropping to $408.6 million from $455.0 million. On Thursday, Children's Place shares closed at $6.59, showing a 3.6% increase. For further details, the complete article is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Children's Place likely to report lower Q1 revenue; analysts expect 51 cents loss per share, $260.62M revenue vs $267.88M last year. Stock closed at $6.59.

news.summary: LHV Group recently disclosed its purchase of its own shares on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange on June 5, 2025. This acquisition saw the company securing a significant number of shares, marking a notable transaction in the market.
news.headline: AS LHV Group purchased its own shares on Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange on June 5, 2025.

news.summary: A business is urging customers to get ready for an anticipated above-average Atlantic Hurricane season. The response to Hurricane Helene highlights the significance of being prepared and having a plan in place. This year's Atlantic Hurricane season officially began on June 1, 2025, raising concerns for potential natural disasters. It is essential for individuals to take necessary precautions and prepare ahead of time to mitigate risks. The company emphasizes the importance of readiness given the expected high activity in the Atlantic Ocean in the upcoming months. It is crucial for people to be proactive and have a strategy in place for any possible hurricanes.
news.headline: Georgia Power urges customers to prep for active 2025 hurricane season after Hurricane Helene. Take precautions for readiness and safety.
Eric B. Chin joins Biomerica as Board of Directors and Audit Committee Chair.

news.summary: New data released by the New Jersey Office of the Secretary of Higher Education (OSHE) reveals a significant demand for flexible and inclusive mental health services. The data, gathered from over 78,000 counseling sessions, highlights a growing need for accessible mental health support. This indicates a strong interest and utilization of such services in Trenton, NJ. The findings underscore the importance of offering diverse and adaptable mental health resources to meet the needs of individuals seeking assistance, emphasizing the necessity of ensuring inclusive support systems.
news.headline: New Jersey initiative expands mental health access; 78,000+ sessions show high demand for services.

news.summary: A prominent figure in the open-source community is making enterprise-level secrets management accessible to a wide range of companies, including rapidly growing startups, Fortune 500 firms, and even nation-states. This development was revealed on June 6, 2025, in San Francisco. Infisical, known for its expertise in open-source solutions for secrets, identity, and access management, unveiled a significant investment of $16 million to further their mission.
news.headline: Infisical raises $16M in Series A round to revolutionize enterprise secrets, identity, and access management.

news.summary: On June 6, 2025, Jianzhi Education Technology Group Company Limited ("Jianzhi") (NASDAQ: JZ), a prominent digital educational content provider in China, reiterated its strong market position in the rapidly changing Chinese digital education sector. Jianzhi stands out for its innovative approach and commitment to meeting the growing demand for high-quality educational resources in China. The company's dedication to excellence has enabled it to capture a significant share of the market, making it a key player in the industry.
news.headline: Jianzhi in China’s digital education market reaffirms unique positioning (June 6, 2025) with growth in NASDAQ: JZ.

news.summary: Following the USDA's recent decision, Cibus can now proceed with the development of disease-resistant traits in Canola without regulation. This move propels Cibus forward in the Canola market, a key crop worldwide.
news.headline: USDA deems Cibus' HT2 trait in Canola as not regulated, along with disease resistance traits, boosting Canola product development.

news.summary: The global natural gas market is projected to grow from $4.1 trillion in 2024 to nearly $6.5 trillion by 2034, showing a CAGR of approximately 5.0%. This growth is driven by a global transition to cleaner energy sources, technological advancements, and rapid industrialization. The market is segmented into conventional and unconventional natural gas, LNG, by end-use applications (power generation, industrial use, residential, commercial, transportation), distribution methods (pipelines, LNG carriers, CNG) and regions (North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Middle East, Latin America, Africa). Unconventional natural gas, including shale gas, tight gas, and coalbed methane, contributes around 55% of new resources. LNG represents about 30% of global trade, with Asia and Europe being key drivers. Power generation is the largest sector consuming 40% of natural gas, followed by industrial use (30%), residential (20%), commercial (10%), and transportation (5%). Pipelines handle over 70% of distribution, LNG carriers 25%, and CNG 5%. North America leads the market with 36% of global revenue, followed by Europe (24%) and Asia-Pacific (28%). Europe is expected to grow at a 3.5% CAGR, while Asia-Pacific is forecasted to expand at a 4.2% CAGR.
news.headline: Global natural gas market expected to reach USD 6.5 trillion by 2034, growing at 5.0% CAGR. By 2024, market estimated at $4.1 trillion. Dominant regions include North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Major segments include conventional and unconventional natural gas, LNG, and distribution methods like pipelines and LNG carriers. Various end-use applications driving growth, with power generation being the largest segment.SubMenuMarket Insights include North America leading with 36% global revenue contribution in 2024, followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific.

news.summary: The cloud-based information governance market is rapidly growing, with its current value at $10 billion in 2024 and predicted to reach $24 billion by 2034, showing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of roughly 9.2% during the forecast period. The surge in demand is fueled by the increasing need for data compliance and security measures in various industries due to the exponential growth of digital data and stricter regulatory frameworks. The market is segmented based on deployment types, solutions, services, end-user industries, organization sizes, and regions. Public cloud solutions are dominant for their cost-effectiveness, while private cloud is crucial for sectors like finance and healthcare with stringent security needs. Hybrid cloud models are gaining popularity for their balance between flexibility and security. Data classification, data discovery, data security, compliance management, and record management solutions are driving market demand. Managed services and professional services are also significant components in supporting information governance. Sectors like healthcare and financial services are at the forefront in adopting cloud-based information governance solutions due to regulatory requirements, while North America leads the market with 42% revenue share, followed by Europe and the Asia-Pacific region with substantial growth potential. Emerging markets in Latin America and the Middle East & Africa show promising prospects despite infrastructural constraints and regulatory inconsistencies.
news.headline: Cloud-based information governance market to reach $24 billion by 2034 at 9.2% CAGR from $10 billion in 2024, driven by data compliance and security demands across industries. Public cloud dominates deployments, while data classification and security solutions lead market demand. North America holds 42% market share in 2024, followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific as fastest-growing region with 15% CAGR. Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) are adopters of cost-effective cloud governance options.

news.summary: The global dual-axis solar tracker market is on the rise, estimated at $1.2 billion in 2024 and projected to reach $3.5 billion by 2034 with a CAGR of 13.3%. Dual-axis trackers offer superior energy capture efficiency compared to fixed systems, making them attractive for modern solar installations. This growth is driven by increasing global commitment to clean energy, technological advancements, and government incentives. Challenges include high upfront costs and maintenance requirements, while emerging trends include intelligent tracking systems, community solar projects, and integration into non-traditional sectors. Market segments include solar panels, controllers, actuators, and sensors, with the residential sector leading in end users and photovoltaic systems dominating in technology. The market is also witnessing growth in commercial, industrial, and utility-scale projects, offering opportunities for further expansion.
news.headline: Global dual-axis solar tracker market to reach $3.5 billion by 2034, growing at 13.3% CAGR driven by demand for sustainable energy and technological advancements. Challenges include high upfront costs and maintenance concerns, with emerging opportunities in intelligent tracking systems and community solar projects. Resegmentations show solar panels contributing over 40% of revenue, with utility-scale projects leading in installation type.

news.summary: The global AI Governance Market saw a valuation of approximately $12 billion in 2024, driven by factors like increased focus on data ethics, regulatory requirements, and the need for compliance frameworks. By 2034, it is forecasted to reach around $36 billion, showing a 12% Compound Annual Growth Rate. The market is divided into Software, Services, and Hardware segments, with software solutions dominating due to their versatile applications in compliance, analytics, and risk mitigation. Key application areas include Risk Management, Data Privacy, Compliance, Cybersecurity, and Analytics, with a growing demand for governance tools that ensure transparency and ethical handling of data. Enterprises, Governments, and SMEs are the main users, with large companies currently leading in AI adoption. North America holds about 40% of the market share, followed by Europe with 25% and Asia-Pacific showing high growth potential. These regions are affected by factors like technological maturity, regulatory frameworks, and government support for ethical AI initiatives.
news.headline: Global AI Governance Market valued at $12 billion in 2024, expected to reach $36 billion by 2034 with a 12% CAGR growth, driven by ethical AI deployment and compliance frameworks. Market divided into Software, Services, and Hardware, primarily catering to Risk Management, Data Privacy, Compliance, Cybersecurity, and Analytics. Key end users are Enterprises, Governments, and SMEs, with North America leading in market share, followed by Europe and the Asia-Pacific region expanding at a CAGR of 8%. Market insights from Exactitude Consultancy reveal increasing demand for AI governance solutions within various industries and regions.

news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc.'s Board of Directors, listed on NYSE and Nasdaq as VZ, has announced a quarterly dividend of 67.75 cents per outstanding share.
news.headline: Verizon board declares 67.75 cents per share dividend on June 6. ($VZ)

news.summary: The Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) market is experiencing substantial growth, with a value of approximately $3.5 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach $12 billion by 2034, showing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of about 14%. This growth is driven by the rising adoption of cloud technologies globally. CSPM solutions help organizations maintain compliance, manage vulnerabilities, and detect threats effectively. Cloud-based CSPM solutions are preferred for their scalability and cost-effectiveness, holding more than 60% of the market share. Large enterprises dominate the market with nearly 70% share, but SMEs are growing rapidly with a surge of over 30% in adoption rates. The BFSI sector leads in CSPM adoption (20%), followed by Retail (15%), IT and telecommunications (18%), Healthcare (12%), and Government (10%). North America leads the market with about 45% revenue share in 2024, benefiting from mature technological infrastructure and widespread cloud adoption.
news.headline: Cloud Security Posture Management market set to hit $12 billion by 2034 with a 14% CAGR. Key trends include AI/ML integration and growing SME adoption. North America leads with 45% revenue share.

news.summary: The global solar cells and modules market was valued at approximately USD 170 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach around USD 420 billion by 2034, growing at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.2%. Monocrystalline solar cells hold 40% of the market share with an efficiency ranging from 15% to 22%. Polycrystalline solar cells, with a market share of 30%, offer an efficiency of 13%-16% at a more affordable price point. Thin-film technology, with a market share of 10%, is lightweight and flexible, ideal for niche markets like Building Integrated Photovoltaics (BIPV). Asia-Pacific leads the market with about 50% of global revenue, followed by North America with a 25% market share, and Europe with a 20% share. The Asia-Pacific region is forecasted to grow at a CAGR of 12%, North America at 8%, and Europe at 7% through 2034. Residential applications lead with 40%, commercial applications hold 25%, and utility-scale projects account for 35% of the market share. Solar modules make up about 70% of the market, serving as the foundational technology for solar power systems. Energy generation is the primary driver of demand, with other key end users being Building Integrated Photovoltaics (BIPV) and electrical & electronic applications. Governments, corporations, and technological advancements are driving the growth of the solar industry.
news.headline: Global solar cells and modules market valued at USD 170 billion in 2024, set to reach around USD 420 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 9.2%. Monocrystalline cells hold 40% market share, residential segment leads with 40% application share. Asia-Pacific leads with 50% revenue share. North America and Europe follow at 25% and 20% respectively.

news.summary: Athens, Greece, June 06, 2025 - Diana Shipping Inc. (NYSE: DSX), a leading global shipping company specializing in dry bulk vessels, has revealed a new time charter agreement for its Ice Class Panamax dry bulk vessel, the m/v Atalandi. The deal, facilitated through a wholly-owned subsidiary, is with Stone Shipping Ltd. The contract entails a gross charter rate of US$9,000 per day for the initial 35 days and US$10,100 per day thereafter, excluding a 5.00% commission to third parties. This agreement is set to last from June 9, 2025, until at least June 15, 2026, with a potential extension until August 15, 2026.
news.headline: Diana Shipping Inc. to charter m/v Atalandi to Stone Shipping Ltd. for $9,000/day for 35 days, then $10,100/day till August 15, 2026.

news.summary: Renowned physician Dr. Howard Grossman has been appointed as the new director at Spectrum Medical Care Center in Phoenix. He is celebrated for his pioneering work in HIV care, championing equitable healthcare for the LGBTQ+ community, and his extensive clinical expertise and leadership. Dr. Grossman's appointment signifies a significant milestone in the medical field, with his years of experience and dedication to advancing healthcare for all.
news.headline: Spectrum Medical appoints LGBTQ+ pioneer Howard Grossman, M.D. as new Medical Director for HIV care and equitable healthcare.

news.summary: Broadcom Inc (NASDAQ: AVGO) posted strong second-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company's second-quarter revenue stood at $15 billion, exceeding the anticipated $14.99 billion. Adjusted earnings per share for the same period were reported at $1.58, beating the projected $1.56 per share. Notably, Q2 AI revenue notably increased by 46% year-over-year, amounting to over $4.4 billion, propelled by the high demand for AI networking. Moving forward, Broadcom anticipates continued growth in AI semiconductor revenue, projecting a rise to $5.1 billion in Q3, thus marking ten consecutive quarters of growth. This expansion is expected to be driven by the company's hyperscale partners. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Analysts increase forecasts for Broadcom post impressive Q2 results; Revenue at $15 billion, beating estimates by $10 million; Adjusted earnings at $1.58 per share, surpassing estimates by $0.02.

news.summary: Iron Mountain and Information Systems Resources are offering complimentary document shredding and electronics recycling services at Comerica's Great Lakes Campus in Detroit. The event is scheduled for June 6, 2025. Comerica Bank invites individuals to bring their paper documents as well as computer equipment and accessories for disposal. This initiative aims to promote responsible waste management by providing a convenient and eco-friendly solution.
news.headline: Comerica Bank's Metro Detroit Shred Day offers free shredding & recycling on June 14 at Great Lakes Campus.

news.summary: Renowned beauty brand Charlotte Tilbury has exciting news to share as they announce a groundbreaking partnership with the iconic Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders (DCC). Serving as the official beauty partner of the DCC, Charlotte Tilbury will infuse its signature confidence-boosting magic and unparalleled Tilbury feeling into the beloved American franchise. This collaboration aims to bring a sense of empowerment and glamour to the DCC, with the partnership set to reach a global audience of fans.
news.headline: New partnership: Charlotte Tilbury teams with Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders for beauty deal, boosting confidence globally.

news.summary: In Latin America, crypto adoption is rapidly increasing, with on-chain value reaching $415 billion in 2024, a 42.5% year-over-year increase, making it the second-fastest-growing crypto market globally. By early 2025, there were an estimated 55 million crypto users in the region, with 95% planning to further expand their exposure in the same year. Stablecoins play a central role in crypto usage in Latin America, with 90% of crypto flows in Brazil associated with stablecoins, primarily used for remittances and inflation hedging. Regulatory efforts are also underway, with Brazil focusing on stablecoin oversight and licensing frameworks, while piloting its Drex central bank digital currency (CBDC). Argentina launched a national registry for virtual asset service providers (VASPs) in 2024 to improve transparency and comply with FATF guidelines. However, despite the growing crypto ecosystem, three-quarters of Latin American crypto media outlets saw a decline in traffic between January and March 2025. The report raises questions about the reasons for this trend and aims to understand the disconnect between the growth of crypto and the struggles of media outlets meant to support it.
news.headline: LATAM crypto media traffic decreased in Q1 2025 despite rising adoption. $415 billion on-chain value in 2024, 55 million users, 95% planning to grow exposure. 90% of Brazil's crypto flows linked to stablecoins. Regulatory efforts increasing in Brazil and Argentina. 75% of LATAM outlets saw declining traffic in Q1 2025. Why are media outlets struggling as crypto adoption grows in the region?
